name: Kuwait
summary: Day 96 (Wednesday June 3; covers 2 June afternoon -> 3 June morning). **KUWAIT TOOK THE CYCLE'S FIRST FATAL HIT AND BROKE WITH TEHRAN — AN IRANIAN DRONE STRUCK KUWAIT INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT'S TERMINAL, KILLING ONE CIVILIAN (AN INDIAN NATIONAL) AND INJURING 63, AND KUWAIT EXPELLED TWO IRANIAN DIPLOMATS.** In the pre-dawn 3 June Iranian barrage (~30 projectiles across the Gulf), a drone hit Kuwait International Airport's passenger terminal (T1), killing one person, injuring 63 (7 critical), causing 'significant material damage' and damaging diplomatic missions (Kuwait FM/health ministry). The single confirmed fatality was an Indian national; several other Indians were among the injured. Kuwait's deputy FM summoned Iran's chargé d'affaires, declared two embassy staff persona non grata (24h to leave), and condemned 'the criminal Iranian aggression' that targeted 'vital and civilian infrastructure, including Kuwait International Airport.' The IRGC's claim that it hit the Ali Al Salem Air Base (the actual US-military target) was part of a target list CENTCOM rejected as FALSE — i.e. Iran aimed at US forces, missed, and the lethal damage fell on a Kuwaiti civilian terminal. This is a hard shift: Kuwait, previously a low-profile US-forces host and quiet GCC partner, is now in open diplomatic rupture with Tehran after taking casualties — degrading the regional mediation environment exactly as the Iran-US channel was suspended. Watch whether Bahrain/Qatar/UAE follow with condemnations or hold mediator-neutral. **No Kuwait trigger; significance = first lethal consequence of the cycle, Gulf-mediator-host rupture.** PRIOR — Day 95 (Monday June 2; covers 30 May afternoon -> 2 June morning). **KUWAIT WAS DIRECTLY TARGETED AGAIN — IRAN FIRED TWO BALLISTIC MISSILES AT US FORCES IN KUWAIT (SUN NIGHT 31 MAY/~11pm ET), BOTH INTERCEPTED, NO CASUALTIES — A GRADUATED WIDENING FROM THE SINGLE 28 MAY SHOT.** Per CENTCOM (Monday post, corroborated by CNBC/Bloomberg/OANN/Washington Times), Iran launched two ballistic missiles at US forces based in Kuwait; both were intercepted with no injury to US personnel. This came amid the broader 30 May-1 June kinetic exchange (US self-defence strikes on Goruk/Qeshm; Iran's MQ-1 shootdown) and Iran's 1 June suspension of talks. Iran did NOT officially comment on the Kuwait launch; some Iranian framing claimed retaliation 'via an air base attack.' Significance: a directly-struck Gulf US-base host for the second time this cycle, with the target SET widened from one to two missiles — but still the 2020 Ain al-Assad/28 May calibrated-intercept template (no casualties), escalation-as-leverage not war (Sadjadpour). The Iranian salvo also puts a Gulf mediator-ecosystem state directly in the kinetic frame even as the Iran-US channel it helped facilitate was suspended. **No Kuwait trigger; significance = calibrated/contained, not escalation-to-war.** PRIOR — Day 92 (Saturday May 30). **NO NEW KUWAIT-DIRECTED KINETIC EVENT THIS WINDOW — THE 28 MAY IRGC MISSILE (INTERCEPTED OVER KUWAIT, ZERO CASUALTIES) WAS NOT FOLLOWED BY ANY FURTHER STRIKE THROUGH 30 MAY. The new Gulf enforcement event is the US disabling of the Lian Star in the Gulf of Oman (not Kuwaiti airspace). Kuwait remains the site of the last calibrated Iranian missile, now confirmed as a face-saving one-off, not the start of an escalation. No trigger move.** Day 90 (Thursday May 28). **KUWAIT BECAME A DIRECT PARTY TO THE US-IRAN EXCHANGE FOR THE FIRST TIME THIS CYCLE — ITS AIR DEFENSES INTERCEPTED AN IRGC BALLISTIC MISSILE FIRED AT A US AIRBASE ON KUWAITI SOIL AT ~04:50 LOCAL MAY 28. CENTCOM confirmed Iran launched the missile 'toward Kuwait' targeting the 'American airbase, as the origin of the aggression'; Kuwaiti air defenses intercepted it with no damage or casualties; CENTCOM called it an 'egregious ceasefire violation.' Kuwait — previously a silent GCC partner on airspace access — is now the physical venue of an Iranian strike on US forces, notable enough to anchor a dedicated Wikipedia article ('2026 Iranian strikes on Kuwait'). Watch for a Kuwaiti MoFA statement and any GCC condemnation as a tone marker. CRITICAL framing: the strike was telegraphed/intercepted/zero-casualty (the 2020 Ain al-Assad face-saving template), and both Washington and Tehran kept negotiating — NOT a return to full-scale war (Fact-Checker F1).** PRIOR (May 9): Day 71 Saturday. **KUWAIT NAMED IN WSJ SCOOP ON US OFFENSIVE-AIRSPACE ACCESS RESTRICTIONS (alongside Saudi Arabia) — KUWAIT REMAINS PUBLICLY SILENT (UNLIKE SAUDI WHO PUBLICLY DENIED VIA AL-HADATH).** Per the WSJ scoop, Saudi Arabia and Kuwait had denied US base/airspace access for Project Freedom and those restrictions had since been 'lifted' per US officials. Saudi went on record denying via Al-Hadath; Kuwait has not made any public statement. The Kuwaiti silence is the cleanest tell of GCC-coordination on the diplomatic-track posture: Saudi denies publicly while operationally permitting; Kuwait stays silent so no contradiction can be created. PRIOR FRAMING (preserved): Kuwait included in India-evacuation architecture; April 12 Saudi MoFA protest summons of Iraqi ambassador after Iraqi-militia drone targeting of Kuwait's only civilian airport (named militias Kataib Hezbollah + Asaib Ahl al-Haq per WSJ/ISW); cumulative damage envelope: oil ministry HQ, power plants, 3x desalination plants, airport fuel tanks, refinery; oil exports remain halted under force majeure.

recent_signals.headline: **KUWAIT TOOK THE CYCLE'S FIRST FATAL HIT AND EXPELLED TWO IRANIAN DIPLOMATS.** In the 3 June Iranian barrage, a drone struck Kuwait International Airport's passenger terminal (T1), killing one civilian (an Indian national) and injuring 63 (7 critical), with significant material damage and damage to diplomatic missions. Kuwait's deputy FM declared two Iranian embassy staff persona non grata (24h) and condemned 'the criminal Iranian aggression' on 'vital and civilian infrastructure.' The IRGC claimed it hit the Ali Al Salem Air Base (US-military target) — CENTCOM said that strike failed and rejected the broader battle-damage claims as FALSE; Iran aimed at US forces, missed, and the lethal damage fell on a Kuwaiti civilian terminal. Kuwait — a low-profile US-forces host — is now in open rupture with Tehran after taking casualties, degrading the regional mediation environment as the Iran-US channel is suspended.
sources: Kuwait FM / defence / health ministries (official) — 1 killed, 63 injured, T1 hit, 2 Iranian diplomats expelled
Gulf News (expulsion, persona non grata, 24h); Arab News / Al Jazeera (Kuwait FM condemnation)
CENTCOM — Ali Al Salem airbase strike failed, battle-damage claims FALSE
recent_signals.verification: Airport terminal hit (1 killed, 63 injured) + diplomat expulsion = CONFIRMED ACTION; the dead/injured included Indian nationals (CONFIRMED via India MEA); IRGC 'Ali Al Salem airbase hit' = CLAIMED, CENTCOM says strike failed (no weight on the airbase claim).

recent_signals.headline: **KUWAIT DIRECTLY TARGETED AGAIN — IRAN FIRED TWO BALLISTIC MISSILES AT US FORCES IN KUWAIT (31 MAY, SUN NIGHT), BOTH INTERCEPTED, NO CASUALTIES.** CENTCOM (Monday post) said Iran launched two ballistic missiles at US forces based in Kuwait; both intercepted, no injury to US personnel. Part of the broader 30 May-1 June kinetic exchange (US self-defence strikes on Goruk/Qeshm; Iran downed a US MQ-1) and Iran's 1 June talks suspension. Iran did not officially comment on the Kuwait launch. The target set widened from the single 28 May missile to two — a graduated widening of the kinetic map — but the calibrated/intercepted/zero-casualty pattern (Ain al-Assad template) holds: managed escalation, not war. A Gulf mediator-host is now directly in the kinetic frame even as the Iran-US channel it facilitated was suspended.
sources: CENTCOM (Monday post, two ballistic missiles at US forces in Kuwait, intercepted) — official military
CNBC / Bloomberg / OANN / Washington Times — independent corroboration
recent_signals.verification: Two Iranian ballistic missiles at US forces in Kuwait + interception, zero casualty = CONFIRMED ACTION (CENTCOM + multi-wire); SIGNIFICANCE = calibrated/contained (Ain al-Assad), target set widened from one to two, NOT escalation-to-war (F1).

recent_signals.headline: **NO NEW KUWAIT-DIRECTED EVENT — 28 MAY INTERCEPTED MISSILE WAS A ONE-OFF (CONFIRMED NON-ESCALATION).** No further strike near Kuwait through 30 May; the new Gulf enforcement action (Lian Star) was in the Gulf of Oman. Kuwait's role this window is the site of the last calibrated, intercepted, zero-casualty Iranian missile.
sources: Iran International liveblog 30 May; Intel dossier 2026-05-30 A1
recent_signals.verification: No new Kuwait-directed kinetic event = CONFIRMED NON-EVENT; 28 May missile confirmed face-saving one-off. No weight change.

recent_signals.headline: **KUWAIT'S AIR DEFENSES INTERCEPTED AN IRGC BALLISTIC MISSILE FIRED AT A US AIRBASE ON KUWAITI SOIL AT ~04:50 LOCAL MAY 28 — KUWAIT IS NOW A DIRECT PARTY TO THE US-IRAN EXCHANGE FOR THE FIRST TIME THIS CYCLE.** CENTCOM confirmed Iran launched a ballistic missile 'toward Kuwait' targeting the 'American airbase, as the origin of the aggression,' in response to a second US 'defensive' strike near Bandar Abbas; Kuwaiti air defenses intercepted it with no damage or casualties; CENTCOM called it an 'egregious ceasefire violation.' Kuwait had been a silent GCC partner on airspace access; it is now the physical venue of an Iranian strike on US forces. The strike was calibrated/intercepted/zero-casualty (Ain al-Assad mode) and both sides kept negotiating — NOT a return to full-scale war. Watch for a Kuwaiti MoFA statement and any GCC condemnation as a tone marker.
sources: CENTCOM (ballistic missile toward Kuwait, intercepted, 'egregious ceasefire violation') — official military
Kuwaiti military / Kuwait MoD (interception confirmed) — official
recent_signals.verification: Iran ballistic missile fired at US airbase on Kuwaiti soil + Kuwaiti interception, zero casualty = CONFIRMED ACTION (CENTCOM + Kuwait MoD, multi-wire); SIGNIFICANCE = calibrated/contained (Ain al-Assad), NOT escalation-to-war (F1).

recent_signals.headline: KUWAIT NAMED IN WSJ SCOOP ON US OFFENSIVE-AIRSPACE ACCESS RESTRICTIONS (alongside Saudi Arabia) — KUWAIT REMAINS PUBLICLY SILENT WHILE SAUDI WENT ON RECORD DENYING VIA AL-HADATH. Cleanest tell of GCC coordination on diplomatic-track posture: Saudi denies publicly while operationally permitting; Kuwait stays silent so no contradiction can be created. No Iranian kinetic action against Kuwait-bound vessels in cycle.
recent_signals.verification: Kuwait public silence = CONFIRMED via absence; WSJ underlying scoop = CONFIRMED REPORTED via wire

historical_summaries.summary: 1 signals. Iranian missiles and drones targeted Ali Al Salem Air Base and US Embassy in Kuwait City

4 signals. Drones struck two Kuwaiti refineries, contributing to oil price spike above $112/bbl | Air raid sirens activated across Kuwait early Sunday March 29 | Iranian drone/missile attack struck a power and water desalination plant — 1 Indian worker killed, significant material damage to service bu

12 signals. Iranian drone struck Kuwait-flagged VLCC Al-Salmi at Dubai's port anchorage zone on March 31 — a fully laden vessel carrying approximately 2 | Iranian drone attack struck fuel tanks at Kuwait International Airport on April 1, setting them ablaze in a 'massive blaze | SECOND water infrastructure strike in 4 days: Iran targeted Kuwait water desalination plant AND oil refinery on April 3
